citation.anchor: Key Miss and Permission faults are only raised when memory translations are enabled

preconditions: The selected instruction or data translation is present with a nonzero 24-bit key
PSR.it or PSR.dt and PSR.pk are enabled for faulting cases
The translated interruption-handler page has a distinct permissive key when PSR.it and PSR.pk remain enabled
inputs: Read-disable, write-disable, and execute-disable PKR images
A valid nonmatching key for instruction and data references
The same denied images with PSR.pk clear
expected_results: Read, write, and execute disables raise Key Permission with exact access metadata
Missing keys raise the instruction or data Key Miss vector
The handler installs a permissive key, serializes, returns with RFI, and the original access completes once
PSR.pk clear bypasses key checking for translated data and instruction references
allowed_results: The diagnostic record may retain the repaired interruption after successful RFI completion
permitted_state_changes: Interruption state and declared result registers
required_unchanged_state: Guest code outside the declared handler and target pages
cases.committed_prefix: Setup translations and PKRs commit before the fault; the denied reference commits no data or instruction effect
cases.restart_action: Install the matching permissive PKR, issue the required serialization, and RFI retries the original reference exactly once

result_observations: exact exception kind, vector, CR.IIP, CR.IFA, and ISR access bits
loaded, stored, or executed result after retry

oracle.derivation: The enable equation, three disable bits, two Key Miss classes, Key Permission class, serialization, and retry expectations are test-owned literals from the cited protection-key contract.

limitations: RSE protection-key references and every cross-fault priority combination remain separate closure units

citation.anchor: max_pkr – Unsigned 8-bit integer denoting the maximum PKR index

preconditions: The PAL static firmware gate is available
The product profile implements sixteen PKRs, eight DTRs, and eight ITRs
inputs: Each reserved argument independently set to one
expected_results: The valid call returns the exact two VM information words
Maximum PKR, DTR, and ITR indices are 15, 7, and 7 rather than register counts
Each nonzero reserved argument returns -2 with zero payloads
allowed_results: No alternative product profile is permitted for this declared synthetic CPU
required_unchanged_state: Guest memory and unrelated architectural state
cases.committed_prefix: Each call publishes one complete status and payload tuple
cases.restart_action: Each call returns directly without retry

oracle.derivation: The procedure index, bit layout, maximum-index convention, three reserved arguments, and all declared product values are test-owned literals from the cited PAL contract and independently exercised register banks.

limitations: PAL_VM_INFO and PAL_VM_PAGE_SIZE are separate discovery contracts

citation.anchor: For all register files other than the region registers, bits {7:0} of GR r3 are used as the index

preconditions: Execution is at CPL0 with PSR.ic enabled and serialized
Selector and value GRs are NaT clear
The General Exception handler validates ISR.code 0x30 and resumes after each faulting bundle
inputs: Distinct valid images for PKR0 through PKR15
Both access directions for every selector 16 through 255
expected_results: All sixteen images survive delayed readback without aliasing
Every nonexistent low-byte selector faults once in each direction
Fault count is 480 with zero ISR and value mismatches
allowed_results: The diagnostic record may retain the final delivered General Exception
required_unchanged_state: Guest code and unrelated architectural state
expected_faults: Reserved Register/Field for each read and write of PKR16 through PKR255
cases.committed_prefix: Implemented writes commit before delayed readback; reserved accesses commit only handler counters
cases.restart_action: Validate ISR.code 0x30, advance CR.IIP one bundle, and RFI for each of 480 faults

result_observations: fault, ISR, and value mismatch counters
terminal IP and final exception record

oracle.derivation: The sixteen-register product boundary, low-eight-bit selector rule, reserved-fault code, 240-by-two count, and distinct images are test-owned constants from the cited instruction contract and product profile.

limitations: Protection checks and field semantics are claimed by separate atomic rows

citation.anchor: Valid – When 1, the Protection Register entry is valid and is checked

preconditions: Instruction and data serialization follow the complete write sequence
inputs: All implemented bits v, wd, rd, xd, and key31:8
Each reserved singleton bit7:4 and bit63:32
expected_results: The all-implemented-bit image reads back exactly
Each of 36 reserved singleton bits raises Reserved Register/Field
Every rejected write leaves the commitment sentinel unchanged
allowed_results: The diagnostic record may retain the final field fault
permitted_state_changes: PKR15 for the valid commitment image
required_unchanged_state: PKR15 across every rejected reserved-field write
expected_faults: Reserved Register/Field for each reserved singleton bit
cases.committed_prefix: The valid sentinel commits after serialization; no reserved-field write commits
cases.restart_action: Validate ISR.code 0x30, advance CR.IIP one bundle, and RFI after each rejected write

oracle.derivation: The v/wd/rd/xd positions, contiguous 24-bit product key, two reserved ranges, exact mask, and serialization requirements are test-owned literals from the cited PKR layout.

limitations: The product implements 24 key bits; larger implementation-dependent key sizes are outside this profile

citation.anchor: The processor ensures uniqueness of protection keys by checking new valid protection keys

preconditions: Writes are followed by the required serialization
inputs: Two valid PKRs with the same key and different disable fields
An invalid PKR with that key
A final valid same-key write to PKR15
expected_results: A new valid key clears only v in every prior valid matching PKR
All other fields in invalidated PKRs remain unchanged
An invalid same-key write does not invalidate the current valid owner
The final valid write invalidates the prior owner and does not alter already-invalid entries
allowed_results: No permission fault occurs because the program performs only PKR moves
required_unchanged_state: All non-v fields of invalidated PKRs
PKRs not participating in the selected key
cases.committed_prefix: Each valid write invalidates prior valid matches before publishing the new PKR image
cases.restart_action: No restart occurs for legal CPL0 PKR writes

oracle.derivation: The valid-only comparison, invalidate-before-write order, v-only mutation, and preservation of invalid historical entries are independent state transitions derived from the cited uniqueness rule.

limitations: Malformed migration images containing duplicate valid keys remain a separate migration-validation unit
